issue: The prefix 'under-' in 'understanding' does not carry its literal spatial meaning (beneath/below) in modern usage — the compound is fully lexicalized and 'understand' is no longer analysed as under+stand by native speakers.
suggestion: Consider marking this entry as a frozen compound or simplifying the morpheme breakdown to a single root 'understand' + suffix '-ing'.

issue: The split 'know' + 'ledge' is etymologically debated and '-ledge' is not a productive suffix — the homeLangDetails already acknowledges this. The more defensible boundary may be 'knowl-' + '-edge' or treating the whole as a single root.
suggestion: Consider treating 'knowledge' as a single unanalysable root, or flag as frozen_compound for editorial decision.

issue: The prefix in- in 'injection' is the directional Latin in- (meaning 'into'), but it is homophonous with the negating in- (as in 'inactive'). The canonical glossary maps in- to ne-, which is the negating sense only. A human should decide whether to keep the corrected directional gloss (v-) or add a note distinguishing the two in- prefixes.
suggestion: Keep directional gloss v- for this entry and ensure the glossary distinguishes negating in- (ne-) from directional in- (v-).

issue: The prefix 'in-' in 'information' does not carry its usual directional Latin sense transparently for modern English learners; the word is largely lexicalized and 'in + form + ation' does not reliably compose to its modern meaning.
suggestion: Consider marking this entry with a note that the morpheme decomposition is etymological rather than productive, or treat it as a single root entry.

issue: Outrage ni produktivna angleška sestava out- + rage, temveč je prevzeta iz starofrancoškega outrage (prekoračitev, nasilje). Razdelitev na outrage + -ous je formalno pravilna, a razlaga korena 'outrage' kot samostojne morfeme je za učečega se zavajajoča, če pričakuje kompozicijsko transparentnost.
suggestion: Obravnavaj outrageous kot zamrznjeno sestavo in dodaj pojasnilo v homeLangDetails, da 'outrage' ni 'out' + 'rage', temveč enotna leksikalizirana osnova.

issue: Beseda 'development' je leksikalizirana in njen moderni pomen (razvoj, napredek) se ne izhaja preprosto iz de- + velop + ment. Predpona de- v develop nima zanikovalnega ali smerno-navzdol pomena, ki ga ima sicer.
suggestion: Premisliti, ali je ta beseda primerna za morfemsko sestavljanko v igri, ali jo označiti kot frozen_compound z ustrezno opombo.

issue: Beseda 'entertain' izhaja iz starofrancoščine 'entretenir' (entre + tenir), ne iz 'enter + tain'. Razdelitev na 'enter' + 'tain' je napačna, ker 'enter' tu ni morfem z ločenim pomenom 'vstopiti'.
suggestion: Razmisliti o razdelitvi na 'entre' (med) + 'tain' (držati) + 'ment', ali obravnavati 'entertain' kot nedeljivo osnovo.

issue: Re- v 'require' ne nosi aktivnega pomena ponavljanja za sodobnega govorca — require izhaja iz latinščine requirere, kjer je re- intensifikator, ne pravi ponavljalni predpona. Skupaj z quir je koren praktično leksikaliziran.
suggestion: Razmislite, ali je koristno razstaviti require na re+quir, ali pa obravnavati require kot eno korensko enoto z ement kot priponko.

issue: Re- v 'retire' ne pomeni 'znova' v sodobni angleščini — retire izhaja iz starofrancoščine retirer (umakniti se nazaj), kjer je re- del leksikaliziranega glagola. Sodoben govorec ne zazna 're-' + 'tir' kot produktivno razčlenitev.
suggestion: Razmislite, ali obravnavati retire kot en koren z ement kot priponko, ali ohraniti razčlenitev z jasnim opozorilom, da je re- tu etimološki, ne produktivni predpona.

issue: The canonical glossary maps nunc → -phone, but nunc (Latin nuntiare, to announce/declare) does not mean phone. The gloss -phone (sound/voice device) is semantically wrong for this root and pre-solves the puzzle by pointing directly at the word's meaning. The canonical entry itself appears to be an error.
suggestion: Gloss nunc as a root meaning razglasiti or sporočiti (announce/declare). The canonical glossary entry for nunc should be reviewed and corrected.

issue: The homeLang label '-anec/-anka' uses slash notation listing two alternatives. Per rules, pick one best label and move the alternative to homeLangDetails.
suggestion: Use '-anec' as the label and note '-anka' (feminine form) in homeLangDetails.

issue: The canonical glossary lists 'par' as a morpheme meaning 'pripraviti', but in 'participate' the meaningful root split is 'part' (pars, part) + 'icip' (capere, take) + 'ate'. The current split 'par' + 'ticip' does not align with the actual Latin morpheme boundary pars/part-.
suggestion: Consider splitting as 'part' (del) + 'icip' (-cipir-) + 'ate' (-ati), or review whether the canonical glossary entry for 'par'='pripraviti' applies here at all.

issue: Koren 'pret' in pripona 'et' sta ločena, toda beseda 'interpret' izvira iz latinskega 'interpres' (posrednik, razlagalec) in ni sestavljena iz 'inter' + 'pret' + 'et'. Meja med korenoma je vprašljiva, pripona '-et' pa ni prepoznaven angleški morfem.
suggestion: Razmislite o obravnavi 'interpret' (brez predpone mis-) kot enotnega korena, ali pa preverite etimologijo in meje z jezikoslovcem.
